steps.title: Identify the appositive elaboration
steps.content: The sentence describes Anderson's action (“chose a narrator who had participated in the protest”) and then elaborates with the phrase “a decision that... amplified the agency.” This phrase restates the same action in clarifying terms, making it an appositive structure. Appositives are noun phrases that rename or clarify a preceding noun or action.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content: Appositives are introduced by commas, not periods (which would fragment the sentence) or semicolons (which connect independent clauses, not elaborations). D uses a comma after “protest” to introduce the appositive, and the commas around “by allowing the narrative to be shaped by one of their own” set off a parenthetical phrase within the appositive—both correctly done. A, B, and C each mishandle the punctuation. This matches choice D — D is correct.
